ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the grade level */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subject */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the school */,
  "percentage_met_exceeded" REAL /* Percentage met/exceeded for the metric */,
  "assessment_date" TEXT /* Date when the assessment was conducted */,
  "student_count" INTEGER /* Number of students assessed */,
  "additional_indicators" TEXT /* Additional performance indicators in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade level */,
  "grade_name" TEXT /* Name of the grade level (e.g., 3rd Grade, 4th Grade) */,
  "grade_level" INTEGER /* Integer representation of the grade level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject (e.g., Math, Reading) */,
  "subject_category" TEXT /* Category of the subject (e.g., STEM, Humanities) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the district */,
  "demographics" TEXT /* Demographic information about the district */,
  "district_population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "principal_contact" TEXT /* Contact information of the district principal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the state */,
  "state_population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "education_director_contact" TEXT /* Contact information of the state education director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district the school belongs to */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the school */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of the school (e.g., Public, Private) */,
  "student_population" INTEGER /* Number of students in the school */,
  "principal_contact" TEXT /* Contact information of the school principal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schools_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions associated with the user role in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each performance metric

Additional performance indicators in JSON format
